title: brosimum utile subsp. ovatifolium
intro: Brosimum utile subsp. ovatifolium, also known as the Mayan nut tree, is a plant species native to Central America, characterized by its broad, ovate-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Brosimum utile subsp. ovatifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive well-being. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some cases, its seeds and leaves are used in traditional medicine to create tinctures or ointments.
